issue_title: Markdown Bold Not Applying to Korean Text - Android

reasoning_trace.finding: The preferred search tool rg was not installed, so no repository content was read by this command.
The broad repository search found many generated/static-analysis artifacts, including .raven_pa files, so it was too noisy for source-level localization.

reasoning_trace.target: Simplenote/src/main/java and Simplenote/src/main/res for Markdown/preview/bold/WebView patterns
reasoning_trace.finding: The app source points to NoteEditorFragment, NoteMarkdownFragment, SimplenoteEditText, fragment_note_markdown.xml, fragment_note_editor.xml, and markdown CSS assets as the relevant preview-rendering path.
NoteMarkdownFragment owns the preview WebView and updateMarkdown(String), which loads HTML generated by getMarkdownFormattedContent(String, String).
NoteEditorFragment routes editor content into preview via loadMarkdownData() or mNoteMarkdownFragment.updateMarkdown(mContentEditText.getPreviewTextContent()).
The preview UI is a WebView with id @+id/markdown inside a NestedScrollView, confirming HTML/CSS rendering is used for the Preview tab.
The preview uses CSS assets under Simplenote/src/main/assets, including light_default.css, dark_default.css, and style variants; local font assets only include Roboto Light/Medium/Regular.
All theme CSS files force .note-detail-markdown to font-family: 'Noto Serif', serif; only #title and table headers have explicit font-weight rules; no strong or b selector is defined.
updateMarkdown calls mMarkdown.loadDataWithBaseURL(null, getMarkdownFormattedContent(mCss, text), "text/html", "utf-8", null), so every preview render passes through getMarkdownFormattedContent.

The method builds an HTML header that links https://fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=Noto+Serif, injects the selected CSS, converts Markdown through AndDown.markdownToHtml, and wraps the result in div.note-detail-markdown.

loadMarkdownData calls NoteMarkdownFragment.getMarkdownFormattedContent(mCss, mContentEditText.getPreviewTextContent()) and loads that formatted HTML into the WebView; updateMarkdownView uses the same path for the sibling preview fragment.
getPreviewTextContent() only replaces checklist spans with markdown preview text and otherwise returns content.toString(); it does not alter Korean characters or bold delimiters.
The default light CSS sets .note-detail-markdown { font-family: 'Noto Serif', serif; } and has no rule ensuring bold weight for strong/b text.
The default dark CSS has the same font-family and missing strong/b font-weight behavior as the light CSS.

reasoning_trace.target: All CSS files for "font-family: 'Noto Serif', serif;" and "font-weight: bold;"
reasoning_trace.finding: Every style variant has the same line forcing Noto Serif at CSS line 44; font-weight:bold appears only for #title, not Markdown bold spans.
ContextUtils.readCssFile loads the selected asset CSS as a string and returns it unchanged for injection into the WebView HTML.
ThemeUtils.getCssFromStyle selects one of the CSS assets; since all contain the same Noto Serif body font, the bug is independent of selected Simplenote style.

reasoning_trace.target: NoteEditorFragment mMarkdown initialization, setMarkdown, showMarkdown, loadMarkdownData
reasoning_trace.finding: On large landscape screens, NoteEditorFragment inflates a WebView, reads CSS with ThemeUtils.getCssFromStyle, and loads formatted Markdown HTML when markdown is enabled or preview is shown.
NotesActivity is the launcher entry point and toggles preview with setMarkdownShowing(), which calls NoteEditorFragment.showMarkdown()/hideMarkdown() on tablet layouts.
NotesActivity is the launcher Activity and NoteEditorActivity is the note editor Activity, confirming the user reproduction starts in those components.
NoteEditorActivity constructs a ViewPager with NoteEditorFragment as the Edit tab and NoteMarkdownFragment as the Preview tab; preview selection displays the NoteMarkdownFragment WebView.
The editor layout contains the note_content SimplenoteEditText; no preview WebView is present in the normal portrait editor layout.
The large-land editor layout includes a stub_webview that inflates fragment_note_webview, explaining the alternate tablet preview path in NoteEditorFragment.
Markdown and preview state are stored as system tags; these flags control whether preview is shown but do not affect character styling.
The Markdown converter dependency is com.commonsware.cwac:anddown:0.4.0; no alternate Markdown renderer is present.
Eliminated as primary cause because it preserves normal text and only changes checklist spans; it cannot selectively remove bold from Korean text.
Eliminated because the Preview tab is visible and Markdown is being rendered; the failure is the visual weight of Korean glyphs, not whether preview is enabled.
Less likely than font injection because an emphasis parse failure would expose literal ** delimiters in preview, while the reported symptom is that preview text is not visually bold.

reasoning_trace.target: Injected Noto Serif font link and CSS body font are incompatible with Korean bold rendering
reasoning_trace.finding: The preview requests generic Noto Serif and forces it for note body text. That family is not Korean-specific and the request does not include a Korean font/weight family, so Korean glyphs fall back in WebView without a visibly bold face for <strong> text.

The most likely triggering statement is the hard-coded Google Fonts Noto Serif link in the generated HTML header; it causes all preview content to use the CSS font stack that fails to render Korean bold visibly.

fault_candidates.reasoning: This statement hard-codes the preview HTML to load generic Noto Serif, while every preview CSS asset forces .note-detail-markdown to font-family: 'Noto Serif', serif. Markdown bold is rendered in the WebView as HTML, but Korean glyphs are not covered by this Latin-oriented font request and fall back without a visibly bold Korean face, so <strong> text appears normal in Preview even though the Markdown delimiters are valid in Edit.

final_answer.causal_chain: The user opens a Markdown note through NotesActivity/NoteEditorActivity. The Preview tab uses NoteMarkdownFragment, or the tablet path calls NoteEditorFragment.loadMarkdownData(), and both call NoteMarkdownFragment.getMarkdownFormattedContent(). That method injects a hard-coded Google Fonts Noto Serif link and the selected CSS into the WebView HTML. The CSS assets force .note-detail-markdown to font-family: 'Noto Serif', serif and do not define a Korean-capable bold rule for strong/b. AndDown converts the Markdown source into HTML for the WebView, but when the generated <strong> content contains Korean characters, WebView falls back from the requested Noto Serif font stack without a visibly bold Korean face. The result is that **Korean text** in Edit becomes non-bold-looking Korean text in Preview.
final_answer.fix_hint: Use a Korean-capable font family and weights in the preview HTML/CSS, such as requesting/including Noto Serif KR or Noto Sans KR with 400 and 700 weights and applying an explicit .note-detail-markdown strong, .note-detail-markdown b { font-weight: 700; } rule.
